blocks.text: Chief Financial Officer Search: Roach, Moss and Hall

items: Client briefing prepared for Jason Myers, Chief Operating Officer
Search team: Sandra Fuentes, Principal; Jeffrey Patterson, Research Consultant; James Grant, Senior Consultant
Retained assignment, commenced {{fact:f:E-2015-001.start}}
Confidential to {{fact:f:E-2015-001.client}}. Not for distribution beyond the interview panel.
Position specification agreed and signed. It has not moved since sign-off and will not without your written agreement.
Market map complete in first pass: the identifiable universe in your sectors and geography, sized and segmented.
Approach calls under way against the signed specification.
It is too early to characterize a slate, and we will not characterize one before we can defend it.

blocks.text: The Market As We Find It

items: The universe that survives the specification is smaller than sector headcount suggests. This is a market of dozens, not hundreds.
Roughly half sit inside firms of your scale or one tier larger. The rest are divisional finance leaders who would be taking a first full officer's seat.
Compensation has moved since the role was last filled. We will bring you evidence rather than impressions.
The scarce trait is not technical accounting. It is the operator who closes fast and can then say what the close means.
Every approach is direct and in confidence, made by James Grant or by Jeffrey Patterson. No advertisement, no posting, no third party.
Candidates hear the sector, the scale, and the shape of the role before they hear the name of the company.
In everything that circulates, including this document, candidates are their current seat and market rather than their name.
Discretion runs both ways: your search is not discussed with a candidate's colleagues, and a candidate's interest never reaches their employer.
Confirm the interview panel and hold provisional dates now. Calendars, not candidates, are what usually costs a search its best person.
A decision in principle on relocation support, ahead of the slate. Two of the strongest segments of this market sit outside your metropolitan area.
Jason Myers to remain the single decision voice to us. Where the panel disagrees, we would rather hear it resolved than averaged.
Approach calls continue against the agreed specification through the coming weeks.
Longlist review, by seat and market, before any candidate is presented to you.
Slate presentation to follow the longlist review, on a date set with your office.
Sandra Fuentes remains your point of contact for anything of substance in the interim.
